title: RED Metrics Dashboard Template (Rate, Errors, Duration)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.7 Splunk APM service-map RED with caller-callee fusion, UC-13.5.16 multi-window SLO burn analytics, UC-13.5.1 pure duration anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 pure error-rate burn, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness integrity, UC-13.5.9 Core Web Vitals RUM, or deep database query analytics in UC-13.5.8. Does not auto-remediate services; it supplies evidence, tiers, and routing hints.
evidence: Saved search obs_uc_13_5_13_red_metrics_template with five-minute schedule; RED dashboard trio cloned per service tier; service_inventory.csv in lookup manager; weekly CSV export archived to restricted index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest otel:traces SERVER spans and matching access logs for a synthetic service, run obs_uc_13_5_13_red_metrics_template, inject ten percent HTTP 500 errors for five minutes, and confirm severity reaches CRITICAL or HIGH when both error and latency multiples exceed inventory targets.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery with healthy spans, sub-one-percent errors, and p99 below twice sli_p99_target_ms for two consecutive buckets, confirm severity returns to LOW for non-canary rows and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to CRITICAL and HIGH only.

dataSources: index=traces sourcetype=otel:traces SERVER spans with duration_nano and HTTP attributes; index=web or index=main sourcetype=access_combined OR access_combined_wcookie OR nginx OR nginx:access OR envoy:access; index=_metrics OpenTelemetry http.server.request.count, http.server.duration, rpc.server.duration per semantic conventions; accelerated Splunk CIM Performance summaries via tstats for process-level cross-check; index=mesh OR istio OR main sourcetype=envoy:access for Istio Envoy or Linkerd-adjacent sidecar access patterns; Splunk Observability Cloud APM RED-style span metrics when mirrored; Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector and Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry; optional Splunk RUM browser request rates; lookup service_inventory.csv with service_name, owner, team, tier, sli_p99_target_ms, sli_error_rate_target, deploy_freeze_until, golden_path_canary, baseline_qps
app: Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry, Splunk Observability Cloud Add-on, optional Istio Add-on for mesh access exports, OpenTelemetry semantic conventions documentation
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.13 RED baseline template: portable Rate-Errors-Duration via macro red_metrics(service). Multisearch arms: OTel SERVER spans, HTTP access logs, OTel mstats, CIM Performance tstats, mesh Envoy. Tunables earliest=-4h@h bin=5m.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=traces sourcetype="otel:traces" span_kind="SERVER" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,upstream_service,""))))
      | eval dur_ms=round(tonumber(duration_nano)/1000000,4)
      | eval is_err=if(tonumber(http_status_code)>=500 OR tonumber('http.response.status_code')>=500 OR like(lower(tostring(status_message)),"%error%") OR match(tostring(otel_trace_status),"ERROR"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS req_ct sum(is_err) AS err_ct perc50(dur_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(dur_ms) AS p95_ms perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_ms BY _time svc
      | eval lane="otel_trace_server"
      | eval err_rate_pct=if(req_ct>0, round(100*err_ct/req_ct,4), 0)
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval prio=1
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ search (index=web OR index=main) (sourcetype="access_combined" OR sourcetype="access_combined_wcookie" OR sourcetype="nginx" OR sourcetype="nginx:access" OR sourcetype="envoy:access") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,upstream_service,""))))
      | eval sc=tonumber(status)
      | eval is_err=if(sc>=500,1,0)
      | eval resp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(response_time,request_time,time_taken,"0"))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS req_ct sum(is_err) AS err_ct perc50(resp_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(resp_ms) AS p95_ms perc99(resp_ms) AS p99_ms BY _time svc
      | eval lane="http_access"
      | eval err_rate_pct=if(req_ct>0, round(100*err_ct/req_ct,4), 0)
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval prio=2
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ | mstats latest(http.server.request.count) AS req_metric latest(http.server.duration) AS dur_latest
        WHERE index=_metrics (metric_name="http.server.request.count" OR metric_name="http.server.duration" OR metric_name="rpc.server.duration") earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m BY service.name metric_name
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | stats sum(req_metric) AS req_sum max(dur_latest) AS dur_pick BY _time svc
      | eval rate_rps=round(tonumber(req_sum)/300,6)
      | eval err_rate_pct=0
      | eval p50_ms=round(tonumber(dur_pick)/1000,4)
      | eval p95_ms=p50_ms
      | eval p99_ms=p50_ms
      | eval req_ct=tonumber(req_sum)
      | eval err_ct=0
      | eval lane="otel_mstats"
      | eval prio=3
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ | tstats count AS dm_ct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=5m Performance.process
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(Performance.process)))
      | eval req_ct=tonumber(dm_ct)
      | eval err_ct=0
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval err_rate_pct=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p95_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval lane="cim_performance"
      | eval prio=4
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ search (index=mesh OR index=istio OR index=main) sourcetype="envoy:access" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',upstream_service,service_name,"mesh_upstream"))))
      | eval sc=tonumber(response_code)
      | eval is_err=if(sc>=500,1,0)
      | eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(total_duration,duration,response_duration,"0"))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS req_ct sum(is_err) AS err_ct perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_ms perc50(dur_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(dur_ms) AS p95_ms BY _time svc
      | eval err_rate_pct=if(req_ct>0, round(100*err_ct/req_ct,4), 0)
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval lane="mesh_envoy_access"
      | eval prio=5
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
| sort 0 + _time + svc + prio
| stats first(rate_rps) AS rate_rps first(err_rate_pct) AS err_rate_pct first(p99_ms) AS p99_ms first(p50_ms) AS p50_ms first(p95_ms) AS p95_ms first(lane) AS source_lane first(req_ct) AS req_ct first(err_ct) AS err_ct BY _time svc
| streamstats time_window=5m current=t global=f avg(rate_rps) AS trail_rate_rps avg(err_rate_pct) AS trail_err_pct avg(p99_ms) AS trail_p99_ms BY svc
| join type=left max=0 svc
    [| inputlookup service_inventory.csv
     | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(service_name)))
     | eval sli_p99_target_ms=tonumber(sli_p99_target_ms)
     | eval sli_error_rate_target=tonumber(sli_error_rate_target)
     | eval baseline_qps=tonumber(baseline_qps)
     | fields svc owner team tier sli_p99_target_ms sli_error_rate_target deploy_freeze_until golden_path_canary baseline_qps ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unowned")
| eval team=coalesce(team,"unknown_team")
| eval tier=coalesce(tier,"tier3")
| eval sli_p99_target_ms=coalesce(sli_p99_target_ms,250)
| eval sli_error_rate_target=coalesce(sli_error_rate_target,0.5)
| eval deploy_epoch=if(len(deploy_freeze_until)>8,strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval in_freeze=if(isnotnull(deploy_epoch) AND _time<=deploy_epoch,1,0)
| eval canary_flag=if(match(tostring(golden_path_canary),"^(1|true|yes)$"),1,0)
| eventstats perc90(p99_ms) AS tier_p90_p99 BY tier
| eval err_over=if(err_rate_pct>(2*sli_error_rate_target),1,0)
| eval lat_over=if(p99_ms>(2*sli_p99_target_ms),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    canary_flag==1, "MEDIUM",
    in_freeze==1, "LOW",
    err_over==1 AND lat_over==1, "CRITICAL",
    err_over==1 OR lat_over==1, "HIGH",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL", "page_owner_compare_red_to_baseline_check_deploy_markers_and_downstream",
    severity=="HIGH", "warn_triage_trace_exemplars_access_logs_and_otel_metrics",
    severity=="MEDIUM", "canary_watch_escalate_if_sustained_across_two_windows",
    true(), "observe_log_to_noise_checklist")
| table _time svc owner team tier source_lane rate_rps err_rate_pct p99_ms trail_rate_rps trail_err_pct trail_p99_ms tier_p90_p99 severity recommended_action sli_p99_target_ms sli_error_rate_target
description: Defines a service-portable RED (Rate, Errors, Duration) monitoring template grounded in Tom Wilkie's RED article and the Google SRE Workbook monitoring chapter. RED fits request-driven microservices where work arrives as synchronous calls; it pairs with USE for resource health and LETS when queues dominate. The use case shows how to derive rate from span counts, access logs, or OpenTelemetry http.server.request.count metrics; errors from span status, HTTP status families, or APM error counters; duration from span duration_nano, log timing fields, or http.server.duration histograms per OpenTelemetry HTTP semantic conventions. It explains standardizing attributes with OpenTelemetry RPC semantic conventions for gRPC, exporting a red_metrics(service) macro, overlaying deploy markers, and keeping thresholds tiered from inventory rather than hard-coding per service. Unlike UC-13.5.7 APM service-map RED fusion, this baseline omits caller-callee topology requirements and unlike UC-13.5.16 it is not an SLO multi-window burn calculator—it is the reusable golden-signal pattern teams clone first.
value: Product and platform leaders get consistent RED panels across hundreds of services so incidents sort by business tier instead of ad hoc chart choices. Engineers shorten triage because rate, failure share, and tail latency share one vocabulary from Tom Wilkie's framing through OpenTelemetry conventions into Splunk dashboards. FinOps and capacity planners can relate infrastructure spend to trailing requests per second once baseline_qps and inventory tiers feed the same template. Risk partners see documented freeze and canary columns so alert suppression stays auditable rather than informal chat agreements. The pattern survives partial migrations: when traces lag, access logs and mstats arms keep RED visible until instrumentation catches up.
implementation: Land OTLP traces with SERVER span_kind, HTTP or gRPC access logs, metrics in _metrics for http.server.request.count and http.server.duration, optional Envoy access from Istio or standalone mesh, and accelerated Performance summaries. Maintain service_inventory.csv with owners, tier, SLI targets, freeze dates, canary flags, and baseline_qps. Save obs_uc_13_5_13_red_metrics_template on a five-minute schedule with earliest=-4h@h, export red_metrics(svc) macro wrapping the same SPL, wire PagerDuty on CRITICAL and HIGH, and tune multisearch index macros to your tenant names.
visualization: Three-panel RED row per service tier: single value rate with sparkline, error share gauge with tier-colored thresholds, duration percentile line with deploy-marker overlays; executive roll-up table keyed on svc with severity coloring and drilldowns to traces, access logs, and metrics lanes.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count AS perf_rows latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_latest FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process
| rename Performance.process AS proc
| table proc perf_rows cpu_latest
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries keyed on Performance.process when correlating host-process observation volume with application RED lanes for services mapped into that object; retain at least thirty days for quarter-over-quarter comparisons.

references.title: Weaveworks blog — The RED Method: key metrics for microservices architecture
Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk APM

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate background batch jobs sometimes log benign 404 responses while discovering shard placement through work-stealing probes; do not treat those as customer errors without route context. Warmup minutes after pod replacement routinely spike p99 while JIT and connection pools settle; require sustained breach across two windows before paging latency. Canary traffic ramps from one percent to five percent can look like a sudden rate spike when denominators are misunderstood; compare against baseline_qps and progressive delivery weights. Enabling host.id style resource detectors without normalization can fan duplicate metric series that inflate rate or split duration; dedupe labels at the collector. Service mesh rolling injections delay sidecar readiness so Envoy-only latency rises while application code is innocent; correlate rollout timestamps. HTTP 499 or client-cancel codes are not server faults yet may be marked errors in naive rules; align policy per API surface. gRPC numeric status codes can disagree with OpenTelemetry span status after SDK upgrades; regression-test mapping tables after bumps. Intentional 429 rate-limit responses are errors for some clients and success for governance; encode product policy in is_err logic. Asynchronous publish semantics may count producer requests while consumers silently drop; pair RED with queue depth monitors. A/B cohort splits can shift measured duration without defects when populations differ; join experiment metadata before rollback.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers, service owners, and site reliability staff who standardize golden signals across request-driven systems. This document is the baseline RED dashboard and alert template: a service-agnostic SPL pattern you export as a macro such as red_metrics(service) so every team reuses the same Rate, Errors, and Duration math while swapping only inventory rows and index macros. The RED method, articulated by Tom Wilkie for microservices and reinforced in Google site reliability practice, names the three measurements that answer whether a synchronous request path is healthy right now: how many requests per time window, what share fail against policy, and how long the slow tail takes. RED complements USE, which focuses on resource saturation and errors for hosts, and LETS, which highlights queue length, errors, duration, and throughput when queueing dominates. Together they prevent blind spots: a service can look green on CPU while collapsing on dependency timeouts, or look busy on CPU while idle on requests.

OpenTelemetry is the normalization spine. HTTP semantic conventions define http.server.request.count and http.server.duration so histogram percentiles remain comparable across Java auto-instrumentation, Python exporters, Node.js agents, and Go SDKs. RPC conventions cover rpc.server.duration for gRPC services. Span kind SERVER marks inbound work for a service instance; CLIENT marks outbound calls. Template consumers must keep that distinction crisp so rate denominators reflect customer-visible entry points rather than every internal hop. Auto-instrumentation in each language attaches resource attributes; verify service.name is set at the SDK or collector, not invented at search time.

Service mesh telemetry from Istio Envoy access logs or Linkerd Prometheus metrics adds sidecar-level rate and error context when applications lack native spans. The Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector and Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry carry OTLP into traces and metrics indexes Splunk can search. Splunk Observability Cloud already materializes RED-friendly span metrics for APM views; this template mirrors that thinking inside Splunk Enterprise for teams that centralize evidence in indexed data. Optional Splunk Real User Monitoring supplies browser-side rate when you extend RED to page-load journeys, keeping the same percentile discipline.

Maintain service_inventory.csv with columns service_name, owner, team, tier, sli_p99_target_ms, sli_error_rate_target, deploy_freeze_until, golden_path_canary, and baseline_qps. Owners attest targets quarterly. Deploy-marker overlays come from your continuous delivery tool: stamp build_id or change_ticket on spans or parallel lookup so dashboards correlate RED shifts with releases. Alert tier policy maps CRITICAL to simultaneous breach of error and latency multiples, HIGH to a single breach, MEDIUM to canary windows, and LOW during documented change freezes so paging stays honest.

Differentiation from sibling catalog entries keeps reviewers aligned. UC-13.5.1 stays duration-anomaly-first on trace tails. UC-13.5.2 stays error-rate-first with burn framing. UC-13.5.3 stays completeness and propagation integrity. UC-13.5.5 stays log-trace correlation. UC-13.5.6 stays fan-out depth. UC-13.5.7 stays Splunk APM service-map-grounded RED with caller-callee fusion. UC-13.5.8 stays datastore query latency. UC-13.5.9 through UC-13.5.11 stay RUM and synthetic viewpoints. UC-13.5.12 stays detector health. UC-13.5.16 stays multi-window SLO burn. UC-13.5.19 stays cardinality governance. This UC-13.5.13 is the portable RED skeleton: multisource fusion, inventory thresholds, macro export, and golden-signal tiering without requiring the APM map context that UC-13.5.7 mandates.

Legal and privacy review covers high-cardinality labels on metrics and spans. Strip or hash user identifiers at the collector when counsel requires minimization. Restrict indexes to engineering identities with job need. Document retention for traces versus logs versus metrics so Finance understands storage posture.

Risk briefing: a template is not a substitute for service knowledge. Low traffic services produce noisy error percentages; pair with baseline_qps floors before paging. Multi-region failover can shift rate without defects; compare regions before rollback. Histogram bucket changes can move percentiles without code edits; version your OpenTelemetry collector and exporter together.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane one indexes OpenTelemetry trace spans with sourcetype otel:traces and span_kind SERVER so each row represents inbound service work. Duration for search comes from duration_nano converted to milliseconds for human thresholds. Error flags combine HTTP response codes from attributes, span status strings, and defensive message scans so partially migrated SDKs still contribute signal.

Lane two harvests HTTP and gRPC friendly access logs including access_combined, access_combined_wcookie, nginx, and envoy access formats. Status codes drive error numerator; response_time style fields drive duration percentiles when present. When Common Log Format lacks timing, prefer trace or metrics lanes for duration and use logs mainly for error codes.

Lane three queries metrics indexes with mstats on OpenTelemetry HTTP and RPC names. Histogram exports store duration in native units; convert to milliseconds at search time for alignment with trace-derived milliseconds. mstats scales better for high-volume services than raw span scans because the metrics pipeline pre-aggregates. For each five minute bucket, sum request counter increments and pick representative duration points; advanced teams can replace latest with percentile aggregations their tenant supports.

Lane four uses tstats against the Performance data model as a host and process anchored cross-check. It does not replace application RED but catches situations where process-level observation counts move while application instrumentation is missing, prompting instrumentation fixes.

Lane five ingests mesh Envoy access logs for sidecar-visible rate and errors when applications run inside Istio or similar meshes. coalesce on service.name, service_name, and upstream_service unifies identities across lanes before joins.

After multisearch, sort by priority so otel_trace_server wins over http_access, then mstats, then Performance, then mesh. That ordering prevents double counting when multiple lanes see the same requests. streamstats with a five minute time window builds trailing averages for rate, error share, and tail latency per service. join brings inventory targets. eventstats computes tier percentile context for peer comparison. case applies CRITICAL when error_rate_pct exceeds twice sli_error_rate_target and p99_ms exceeds twice sli_p99_target_ms, HIGH when either breaches, MEDIUM when golden_path_canary marks an intentional experiment path, and LOW during deploy_freeze_until windows parsed as dates. recommended_action gives terse runbook routing strings for paging systems.

Duration percentiles from spans use exact span durations per request in the bucket. Duration from mstats reflects histogram or exported summary points; when only a mean-like statistic exists, p50 and p99 collapse to the same estimate until you enable richer histogram export. Document which lane is authoritative per environment.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as obs_uc_13_5_13_red_metrics_template with a five minute schedule in production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH after excluding rows where baseline_qps implies a too-small denominator. Throttle duplicate service and window tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Wrap the SPL in a macro red_metrics(svc_glob) that injects a matching clause on svc so dashboards parameterize one implementation.

Pipeline walkthrough begins with comment macro documentation for operators. multisearch fans five arms described in Step 2. Each arm emits compatible fields before priority-aware first-value stats collapse duplicates per bucket. streamstats time_window=5m smooths short spikes. join type=left enriches from service_inventory.csv. eventstats adds tier_p90_p99 peer context. case encodes severity and recommended_action. Closing table lists seventeen analyst-facing columns for dashboards and exports.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.13 RED baseline template: portable Rate-Errors-Duration via macro red_metrics(service). Multisearch arms: OTel SERVER spans, HTTP access logs, OTel mstats, CIM Performance tstats, mesh Envoy. Tunables earliest=-4h@h bin=5m.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=traces sourcetype="otel:traces" span_kind="SERVER" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,upstream_service,""))))
      | eval dur_ms=round(tonumber(duration_nano)/1000000,4)
      | eval is_err=if(tonumber(http_status_code)>=500 OR tonumber('http.response.status_code')>=500 OR like(lower(tostring(status_message)),"%error%") OR match(tostring(otel_trace_status),"ERROR"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS req_ct sum(is_err) AS err_ct perc50(dur_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(dur_ms) AS p95_ms perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_ms BY _time svc
      | eval lane="otel_trace_server"
      | eval err_rate_pct=if(req_ct>0, round(100*err_ct/req_ct,4), 0)
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval prio=1
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ search (index=web OR index=main) (sourcetype="access_combined" OR sourcetype="access_combined_wcookie" OR sourcetype="nginx" OR sourcetype="nginx:access" OR sourcetype="envoy:access") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,upstream_service,""))))
      | eval sc=tonumber(status)
      | eval is_err=if(sc>=500,1,0)
      | eval resp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(response_time,request_time,time_taken,"0"))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS req_ct sum(is_err) AS err_ct perc50(resp_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(resp_ms) AS p95_ms perc99(resp_ms) AS p99_ms BY _time svc
      | eval lane="http_access"
      | eval err_rate_pct=if(req_ct>0, round(100*err_ct/req_ct,4), 0)
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval prio=2
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ | mstats latest(http.server.request.count) AS req_metric latest(http.server.duration) AS dur_latest
        WHERE index=_metrics (metric_name="http.server.request.count" OR metric_name="http.server.duration" OR metric_name="rpc.server.duration") earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m BY service.name metric_name
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | stats sum(req_metric) AS req_sum max(dur_latest) AS dur_pick BY _time svc
      | eval rate_rps=round(tonumber(req_sum)/300,6)
      | eval err_rate_pct=0
      | eval p50_ms=round(tonumber(dur_pick)/1000,4)
      | eval p95_ms=p50_ms
      | eval p99_ms=p50_ms
      | eval req_ct=tonumber(req_sum)
      | eval err_ct=0
      | eval lane="otel_mstats"
      | eval prio=3
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ | tstats count AS dm_ct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=5m Performance.process
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(Performance.process)))
      | eval req_ct=tonumber(dm_ct)
      | eval err_ct=0
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval err_rate_pct=0
      | eval p50_ms=0
      | eval p95_ms=0
      | eval p99_ms=0
      | eval lane="cim_performance"
      | eval prio=4
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
    [ search (index=mesh OR index=istio OR index=main) sourcetype="envoy:access" earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',upstream_service,service_name,"mesh_upstream"))))
      | eval sc=tonumber(response_code)
      | eval is_err=if(sc>=500,1,0)
      | eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(total_duration,duration,response_duration,"0"))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS req_ct sum(is_err) AS err_ct perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_ms perc50(dur_ms) AS p50_ms perc95(dur_ms) AS p95_ms BY _time svc
      | eval err_rate_pct=if(req_ct>0, round(100*err_ct/req_ct,4), 0)
      | eval rate_rps=round(req_ct/300,6)
      | eval lane="mesh_envoy_access"
      | eval prio=5
      | fields _time svc lane prio rate_rps err_rate_pct p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms req_ct err_ct ]
| sort 0 + _time + svc + prio
| stats first(rate_rps) AS rate_rps first(err_rate_pct) AS err_rate_pct first(p99_ms) AS p99_ms first(p50_ms) AS p50_ms first(p95_ms) AS p95_ms first(lane) AS source_lane first(req_ct) AS req_ct first(err_ct) AS err_ct BY _time svc
| streamstats time_window=5m current=t global=f avg(rate_rps) AS trail_rate_rps avg(err_rate_pct) AS trail_err_pct avg(p99_ms) AS trail_p99_ms BY svc
| join type=left max=0 svc
    [| inputlookup service_inventory.csv
     | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(service_name)))
     | eval sli_p99_target_ms=tonumber(sli_p99_target_ms)
     | eval sli_error_rate_target=tonumber(sli_error_rate_target)
     | eval baseline_qps=tonumber(baseline_qps)
     | fields svc owner team tier sli_p99_target_ms sli_error_rate_target deploy_freeze_until golden_path_canary baseline_qps ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unowned")
| eval team=coalesce(team,"unknown_team")
| eval tier=coalesce(tier,"tier3")
| eval sli_p99_target_ms=coalesce(sli_p99_target_ms,250)
| eval sli_error_rate_target=coalesce(sli_error_rate_target,0.5)
| eval deploy_epoch=if(len(deploy_freeze_until)>8,strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval in_freeze=if(isnotnull(deploy_epoch) AND _time<=deploy_epoch,1,0)
| eval canary_flag=if(match(tostring(golden_path_canary),"^(1|true|yes)$"),1,0)
| eventstats perc90(p99_ms) AS tier_p90_p99 BY tier
| eval err_over=if(err_rate_pct>(2*sli_error_rate_target),1,0)
| eval lat_over=if(p99_ms>(2*sli_p99_target_ms),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    canary_flag==1, "MEDIUM",
    in_freeze==1, "LOW",
    err_over==1 AND lat_over==1, "CRITICAL",
    err_over==1 OR lat_over==1, "HIGH",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL", "page_owner_compare_red_to_baseline_check_deploy_markers_and_downstream",
    severity=="HIGH", "warn_triage_trace_exemplars_access_logs_and_otel_metrics",
    severity=="MEDIUM", "canary_watch_escalate_if_sustained_across_two_windows",
    true(), "observe_log_to_noise_checklist")
| table _time svc owner team tier source_lane rate_rps err_rate_pct p99_ms trail_rate_rps trail_err_pct trail_p99_ms tier_p90_p99 severity recommended_action sli_p99_target_ms sli_error_rate_target
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[obs_uc_13_5_13_red_metrics_template_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch obs_uc_13_5_13_red_metrics_template | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")
```

Performance guidance: materialize five minute summaries per svc when Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time rising; widen operational alerts to read summaries while retaining this search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Deploy a synthetic OpenTelemetry instrumented service in a lab namespace. Generate steady load near one hundred requests per second for twenty minutes and confirm rate_rps, err_rate_pct, and p99_ms stabilize near expected baselines in the template output. Inject ten percent HTTP 500 responses for five minutes and confirm severity rises to CRITICAL or HIGH consistent with inventory multiples. Restore healthy responses and confirm severity falls within two buckets. Inject artificial latency so p99_ms exceeds twice sli_p99_target_ms while errors stay low and confirm HIGH latency path. Overlay deploy markers from your pipeline and verify rows annotate the change window.

Ground truth compares the template to Observability Cloud RED tiles or raw metric explorers when mirrored feeds exist. Validate inventory join keys by running inputlookup service_inventory.csv alone and confirming svc coverage for tier-one services. Negative test on a quiet window should yield LOW or MEDIUM only for flagged canary rows.

Runbook linkage ties recommended_action tokens to concrete tools: trace explorer for exemplars, log filters for status trails, mesh dashboards for sidecar resets, and cost views that divide infrastructure spend by trailing rate for FinOps reviews.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — mstats aggregation skewed by histogram bucket boundary: percentile jumps after exporter upgrade without code changes; compare histogram bucket series, align collector versions, and temporarily trust trace percentiles until metrics stabilize.

Case B — span_kind missing because tracing SDK not migrated: SERVER spans disappear into generic kinds; fix instrumentation, then revalidate rate denominators against access logs.

Case C — OpenTelemetry resource detector enabling host.id duplicates series: shards split one logical service; normalize resource attributes at collector and dedupe in inventory.

Case D — rate-limited 429 responses counted as errors inappropriately: business policy treats throttle as success; adjust is_err logic for chosen routes.

Case E — gRPC code mapping mismatch with OpenTelemetry SDK version: numeric gRPC status disagrees with semantic span status; align exporter tables and regression-test status mapping after upgrades.

Case F — asynchronous publisher pattern producing zero RED: work leaves the request path before completion; extend monitoring with queue depth metrics and outbox patterns rather than HTTP span alone.

Case G — mesh sidecar latency overshadows application latency: Envoy p99 exceeds app p99; investigate listener config, TLS handshake, and subset load balancing before blaming service code.

Case H — access log Common Log Format missing duration field: duration percentiles flatten to zero; rely on trace or metrics lanes and downgrade log-only latency claims.

Case I — auto-instrumentation agent crash silently: spans stop while metrics continue; watch collector health signals and process restarts alongside this search.

Case J — service.name not set on collector: svc becomes blank or literal unknown; enforce resource processors and reject deployments missing identity in pre-merge checks.

Case K — canary tier mislabeled in inventory CSV: golden_path_canary flips severity for stable services; audit CSV quarterly and automate schema validation in Git.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, mstats, and tstats appear; final table lists operational columns including owner, tier, and recommended_action.
grandmaExplanation: We count how much work arrives, how often it fails, and how long the slowest responses take. When those three jump past the written guardrails for a service, we sound the alarm so the owning team fixes it before most customers notice.
hardware: Applies to Linux and containerized service fleets exporting OTLP to Splunk, Kubernetes workloads with Istio or Linkerd sidecars, and hybrid estates mirroring Observability Cloud metrics into Splunk Enterprise.
